1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// RecoveryFailureClass categorises the persistent failure mode that exhausted
9	// the escalation ladder, so the PostLadderExhaustionHook can choose the
10	// appropriate recovery action.
11	type RecoveryFailureClass string
12	
13	const (
14		// SpecGap is set when the last attempt was blocked by a spec-gap or
15		// missing-acceptance review verdict.
16		SpecGap RecoveryFailureClass = "spec_gap"
17		// TooLarge is set when the last attempt was blocked by a too-large review
18		// verdict.
19		TooLarge RecoveryFailureClass = "too_large"
20		// PersistentExecutionFailed is the default class for all other persistent
21		// failures.
22		PersistentExecutionFailed RecoveryFailureClass = "persistent_execution_failed"
23	)
24	
25	// RecoveryPath indicates which automated recovery action a
26	// PostLadderExhaustionHook took.
27	type RecoveryPath string
28	
29	const (
30		Reframe   RecoveryPath = "reframe"
31		Decompose RecoveryPath = "decompose"
32	)
33	
34	// PostLadderExhaustionResult is the outcome returned by a
35	// PostLadderExhaustionHook invocation.
36	type PostLadderExhaustionResult struct {
37		Attempted bool
38		Succeeded bool
39		Path      RecoveryPath

43	// PostLadderExhaustionHook is called when the consecutive_ladder_exhaustions
44	// counter for a bead reaches the auto-recovery threshold (>= 2). It should
45	// attempt automated recovery and return the outcome. A nil hook or a result
46	// with Attempted=false causes the caller to fall through to the existing loop
47	// path unchanged.

50	// deriveRecoveryFailureClass maps the last-attempt report to a
51	// RecoveryFailureClass for use by the PostLadderExhaustionHook.
52	func deriveRecoveryFailureClass(report ExecuteBeadReport) RecoveryFailureClass {
53		switch {
54		case strings.Contains(report.Status, ReviewTerminalClassSpecGap),
55			strings.Contains(report.Status, ReviewTerminalClassMissingAcceptance):
56			return SpecGap
57		case strings.Contains(report.Status, ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge):
58			return TooLarge
59		default:
60			return PersistentExecutionFailed

102	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
103		mode := r.Mode
104		if mode == "" {
105			if r.Once {
106				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
107			} else {
108				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
109			}
110		}
111		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
112		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
113			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
114		}
115		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
116			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
117		}
118		return mode, idleInterval
119	}

121	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
122	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
123	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
124	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3

126	// ReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEventKind is the event kind emitted when the
127	// reviewer dispatch is skipped because the implementer produced no commits
128	// (BaseRev == ResultRev). Per ADR-024 P1: cheapest first — paying a reviewer
129	// to evaluate an empty diff is unjustified cost.
130	const ReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEventKind = "reviewer-skipped-empty-diff"

132	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
133	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
134	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
135	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
136	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
137	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
138	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour

140	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
141	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
142	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
143	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
144	// advance the base branch.
145	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
146	
147	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
148	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
149	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
150	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
151	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
152	// worker.
153	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
154	
155	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
156	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
157	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
158	// triage:needs-investigation park.
159	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute

161	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
162	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
163	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
164	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
165	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
166	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
167	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
168	//
169	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
170	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
171	//
172	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
173	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
174	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
175	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
176	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
177	// directly.
178	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
179		ctx context.Context,
180		projectRoot string,
181		b *bead.Bead,
182		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
183		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
184		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
185		actor, source string,
186		now func() time.Time,
187	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
188		if now == nil {
189			now = time.Now
190		}
191		if submit == nil {
192			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
193		}
194		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
195		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
196		if err != nil {
197			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
198			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
199			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
200			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
201			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
202				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
203			}
204			return &LandResult{
205				Status:      "preserved",
206				PreserveRef: ref,
207				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
208			}, nil, nil
209		}
210		if outcome.Blocked {
211			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
212			if perr != nil {
213				return nil, outcome, perr
214			}
215			if eventStore != nil {
216				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
217			}
218			return land, outcome, nil
219		}
220		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
221			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
222		}
223		land, sErr := submit(BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res))
224		return land, outcome, sErr
225	}

227	// handlePostAttemptDecomposition runs the orchestrator-level splitter when a
228	// no_changes attempt signals orchestrator_action: decompose. It checks the
229	// queue-level max_decomposition_depth (not the implementation prompt cap),
230	// validates the AC map for completeness, and either creates children+deps or
231	// parks the parent for operator review if the split is lossy or depth-capped.
232	// The bead must already be unclaimed before this is called.
233	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handlePostAttemptDecomposition(ctx context.Context, candidate *bead.Bead, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, at time.Time) {
234		emit := func(kind string, body map[string]any) {
235			if runtime.EventSink == nil {
236				return
237			}
238			data, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{"event": kind, "payload": body})
239			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.EventSink, "%s\n", data)
240		}
241		parkOperator := func(reason string) {
242			if runtime.Log != nil {
243				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition blocked: %s (%s)\n", reason, candidate.ID)
244			}
245			emit("post_attempt_decomposition.blocked", map[string]any{
246				"bead_id": candidate.ID,
247				"reason":  reason,
248			})
249			if berr := parkBeadPostIntakeRejection(w.Store, candidate.ID, assignee, PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, reason, at); berr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
250				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition park error: %v\n", berr)
251			}
252		}
253	
254		// Queue-level depth check: orchestrator uses its own cap, not the
255		// implementation prompt's hardcoded depth-2 cap.
256		maxDepth := rcfg.MaxDecompositionDepth()
257		if maxDepth > 0 && storeBeadDepth(w.Store, candidate) >= maxDepth {
258			overflowBody, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
259				"depth": storeBeadDepth(w.Store, candidate),
260				"max":   maxDepth,
261			})
262			_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
263				Kind:      "triage-overflow",
264				Summary:   "depth cap exceeded during post-attempt decomposition",
265				Body:      string(overflowBody),
266				Actor:     assignee,
267				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
268				CreatedAt: at,
269			})
270			parkOperator("queue-level depth cap exceeded; operator must split")
271			return
272		}
273	
274		decomp, err := runtime.PostAttemptDecompositionHook(ctx, candidate.ID)
275		if err != nil {
276			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition hook error: %s", err.Error()))
277			return
278		}
279		if decomp == nil {
280			parkOperator("decomposition hook returned no split")
281			return
282		}
283		lossyOrEmpty := isDecompositionLossy(decomp.ACMap) || (len(decomp.ACMap) == 0 && strings.TrimSpace(candidate.Acceptance) != "")
284		if lossyOrEmpty {
285			parkOperator("decomposition AC map is incomplete; operator must produce a lossless split")
286			return
287		}
288	
289		childIDs, decompErr := applyPreClaimDecomposition(w.Store, candidate, decomp, assignee, at)
290		if decompErr != nil {
291			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition apply error: %s", decompErr.Error()))
292			return
293		}
294		if runtime.Log != nil {
295			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition: bead %s split into %s\n", candidate.ID, strings.Join(childIDs, ", "))
296		}
297		emit("post_attempt_decomposition.applied", map[string]any{

1	// Package executeloop defines the canonical spec type for execute-loop workers.
2	// It is import-safe: no dependency on cobra, server, or transport packages.
3	package executeloop
4	
5	import (
6		"encoding/json"
7		"fmt"
8		"strconv"
9		"time"
10	)
11	
12	// SpecCurrentVersion is the single spec version supported by this client and
13	// server. Forward-compat stance: a client MUST set SpecVersion = SpecCurrentVersion;
14	// a server receiving an unknown version SHOULD reject with an error rather than
15	// silently misinterpreting fields. There is no backwards-compat shim — upgrade
16	// client and server together.
17	const SpecCurrentVersion = 1
18	
19	// Mode controls how the execute-loop terminates.
20	type Mode string
21	
22	const (
23		// ModeOnce runs one bead (or none if the queue is empty) then exits.
24		ModeOnce Mode = "once"
25		// ModeDrain processes all ready beads then exits.
26		ModeDrain Mode = "drain"
27		// ModeWatch processes all ready beads, then sleeps for IdleInterval and
28		// rechecks, looping indefinitely until interrupted.
29		ModeWatch Mode = "watch"
30	)
31	
32	// Duration wraps time.Duration with JSON support that marshals as a string
33	// (e.g., "30s") and unmarshals both strings ("30s") and numeric nanoseconds.
34	type Duration struct {
35		time.Duration
36	}
37	
38	func (d Duration) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
39		return json.Marshal(d.String())
40	}
41	
42	func (d *Duration) UnmarshalJSON(b []byte) error {
43		// Try string first.
44		var s string
45		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &s); err == nil {
46			parsed, err := time.ParseDuration(s)
47			if err != nil {
48				return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: invalid duration string %q: %w", s, err)
49			}
50			d.Duration = parsed
51			return nil
52		}
53		// Try numeric nanoseconds.
54		var ns int64
55		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &ns); err == nil {
56			d.Duration = time.Duration(ns)
57			return nil
58		}
59		return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: cannot unmarshal %s as duration (want string or nanosecond integer)", strconv.Quote(string(b)))
60	}

62	// ExecuteLoopSpec is the canonical, transport-agnostic specification for an

116	// ApplyDefaults fills in zero-value fields with their canonical defaults.
117	// Safe to call on a zero-value spec.
118	func (s *ExecuteLoopSpec) ApplyDefaults() {
119		if s.Mode == "" {
120			s.Mode = ModeDrain
121		}
122		if s.Mode == ModeWatch && s.IdleInterval.Duration == 0 {
123			s.IdleInterval = Duration{30 * time.Second}
124		}
125		if s.SpecVersion == 0 {
126			s.SpecVersion = SpecCurrentVersion
127		}
128	}
129	
130	// Validate checks that the spec is well-formed after ApplyDefaults has run.
131	func (s *ExecuteLoopSpec) Validate() error {
132		switch s.Mode {
133		case ModeOnce, ModeDrain, ModeWatch:
134		default:
135			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: unknown mode %q (want once, drain, or watch)", s.Mode)
136		}
137		if s.Mode != ModeWatch && s.IdleInterval.Duration != 0 {
138			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: idle_interval is only valid when mode=watch")
139		}
140		if s.SpecVersion != 0 && s.SpecVersion != SpecCurrentVersion {
141			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: unsupported spec_version %d (want %d)", s.SpecVersion, SpecCurrentVersion)
142		}
143		return nil
144	}

37		case ReviewTerminalClassTooLarge:

### Flow Summary

1. Bead fails with persistent status (per-bead budget or ladder exhaustion)
2. `incrementConsecutiveLadderExhaustions()` increments counter in bead Extra
3. When counter reaches 2, check for `recovery:manual` label:
   - If present: park to proposed without hook
   - Otherwise: invoke `PostLadderExhaustionHook`
4. Hook receives `beadID` and `RecoveryFailureClass` (derived from status)
5. Hook dispatches appropriate recovery:

   - `PersistentExecutionFailed` → `runReframer()`
6. On success: clear counter, emit event, bead returns to open
7. On failure: hook returns `Attempted=true, Succeeded=false`, loop continues

1488	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd *cobra.Command, treatPassthroughAsOpaque bool, tryTargetBeadID string) error {
1489		spec, dispatch, err := parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd, treatPassthroughAsOpaque)
1490		if err != nil {
1491			return err
1492		}
1493		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(spec.ProjectRoot, f.WorkingDir)
1494		spec.ProjectRoot = projectRoot
1495		if tryTargetBeadID != "" {
1496			// `ddx try <bead-id>` is a single-bead one-shot: target one bead and
1497			// exit after one attempt regardless of CLI flag state.
1498			spec.Mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
1499			spec.IdleInterval = executeloop.Duration{}
1500		}
1501	
1502		// Zero-config auto-route: when the operator passes no routing flags and
1503		// the project has no .ddx/config.yaml routing block, dispatch flows
1504		// through fizeau's provider resolution. Provider configuration and any
1505		// "no providers configured" error reporting is fizeau's concern; ddx
1506		// does not read fizeau's config files.
1507		// ADR-022: queue work always runs inline. The legacy --local flag is
1508		// a deprecated no-op and the server-submit path is removed from this CLI
1509		// surface — server-spawned workers exec `ddx work` directly.
1510	
1511		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
1512		// before claiming any beads. This surfaces errors like "claude binary
1513		// not found" or "vidar is an agent preset, not a claude model" before
1514		// any bead status changes hands.
1515		// Skipped for ddx work (treatPassthroughAsOpaque=true): harness/provider/model
1516		// are opaque passthrough constraints there; DDx does not validate them.
1517		if !spec.OpaquePassthrough {
1518			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, spec.Harness, spec.Model, spec.Provider, spec.ModelRef); err != nil {
1519				return fmt.Errorf("execute-loop: %w", err)
1520			}
1521		}

11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
25	constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate these values
26	or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
27	bounds.
28	
29	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
30	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
31	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
32	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
33	
34	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
35	  drained     — no ready beads remain
36	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
37	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
38	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
39	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
40	
41	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
42	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
43	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
44	`,
45			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
46	  ddx work
47	
48	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
49	  ddx work --once
50	
51	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
52	  ddx work --watch
53	
54	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
55	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
56	
57	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
58	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
59	
60	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
61	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
62	
63	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
64	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
65			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
66			RunE: f.runWork,
67		}
68

1	// Package executeloop defines the canonical spec type for execute-loop workers.
2	// It is import-safe: no dependency on cobra, server, or transport packages.
3	package executeloop
4	
5	import (
6		"encoding/json"
7		"fmt"
8		"strconv"
9		"time"
10	)
11	
12	// SpecCurrentVersion is the single spec version supported by this client and
13	// server. Forward-compat stance: a client MUST set SpecVersion = SpecCurrentVersion;
14	// a server receiving an unknown version SHOULD reject with an error rather than
15	// silently misinterpreting fields. There is no backwards-compat shim — upgrade
16	// client and server together.
17	const SpecCurrentVersion = 1
18	
19	// Mode controls how the execute-loop terminates.
20	type Mode string
21	
22	const (
23		// ModeOnce runs one bead (or none if the queue is empty) then exits.
24		ModeOnce Mode = "once"
25		// ModeDrain processes all ready beads then exits.
26		ModeDrain Mode = "drain"
27		// ModeWatch processes all ready beads, then sleeps for IdleInterval and
28		// rechecks, looping indefinitely until interrupted.
29		ModeWatch Mode = "watch"
30	)
31	
32	// Duration wraps time.Duration with JSON support that marshals as a string
33	// (e.g., "30s") and unmarshals both strings ("30s") and numeric nanoseconds.
34	type Duration struct {
35		time.Duration
36	}
37	
38	func (d Duration) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
39		return json.Marshal(d.String())
40	}
41	
42	func (d *Duration) UnmarshalJSON(b []byte) error {
43		// Try string first.
44		var s string
45		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &s); err == nil {
46			parsed, err := time.ParseDuration(s)
47			if err != nil {
48				return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: invalid duration string %q: %w", s, err)
49			}
50			d.Duration = parsed
51			return nil
52		}
53		// Try numeric nanoseconds.
54		var ns int64
55		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &ns); err == nil {
56			d.Duration = time.Duration(ns)
57			return nil
58		}
59		return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: cannot unmarshal %s as duration (want string or nanosecond integer)", strconv.Quote(string(b)))
60	}
61	
62	// ExecuteLoopSpec is the canonical, transport-agnostic specification for an
63	// execute-loop worker. All transports (cobra, REST, GraphQL) map into this type
64	// before persisting or dispatching.
65	type ExecuteLoopSpec struct {
66		ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root,omitempty"`
67		Harness     string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
68		Model       string `json:"model,omitempty"`
69		Profile     string `json:"profile,omitempty"`
70		Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
71		ModelRef    string `json:"model_ref,omitempty"`
72		Effort      string `json:"effort,omitempty"`
73		LabelFilter string `json:"label_filter,omitempty"`
74	
75		// Mode controls loop termination. Defaults to ModeDrain via ApplyDefaults.
76		Mode Mode `json:"mode,omitempty"`
77	
78		// IdleInterval is the sleep duration between queue polls when Mode=watch
79		// and no execution-ready work is found.
80		IdleInterval Duration `json:"idle_interval,omitempty"`
81	
82		NoReview      bool   `json:"no_review,omitempty"`
83		ReviewHarness string `json:"review_harness,omitempty"`
84		ReviewModel   string `json:"review_model,omitempty"`
85	
86		// OpaquePassthrough skips DDx-side route validation and config
87		// harness/model injection (CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010).
88		OpaquePassthrough bool `json:"opaque_passthrough,omitempty"`
89	
90		MaxCostUSD       float64  `json:"max_cost_usd,omitempty"`
91		MaxBeadCostUSD   float64  `json:"max_bead_cost_usd,omitempty"`
92		RequestTimeout   Duration `json:"request_timeout,omitempty"`
93		RateLimitMaxWait Duration `json:"rate_limit_max_wait,omitempty"`
94		MinPower         int      `json:"min_power,omitempty"`
95		MaxPower         int      `json:"max_power,omitempty"`
96	
97		// FromRev, if set, narrows execution to beads introduced after this git revision.
98		FromRev string `json:"from_rev,omitempty"`
99	
100		// SpecVersion must equal SpecCurrentVersion. See SpecCurrentVersion for the
101		// client/server forward-compat stance.
102		SpecVersion int `json:"spec_version,omitempty"`
103	}
104	
105	// DispatchOptions carries control-plane fields used at dispatch time. It is not
106	// persisted into the worker record.
107	type DispatchOptions struct {
108		// Local, when true, runs the worker in-process rather than via the server API.
109		Local bool `json:"local,omitempty"`
110		// JSON is the raw JSON payload to forward when dispatching via REST/GraphQL.
111		// Callers that build the request body manually may set this instead of
112		// populating an ExecuteLoopSpec.
113		JSON string `json:"json,omitempty"`
114	}
115	
116	// ApplyDefaults fills in zero-value fields with their canonical defaults.
117	// Safe to call on a zero-value spec.
118	func (s *ExecuteLoopSpec) ApplyDefaults() {
119		if s.Mode == "" {
120			s.Mode = ModeDrain
121		}
122		if s.Mode == ModeWatch && s.IdleInterval.Duration == 0 {
123			s.IdleInterval = Duration{30 * time.Second}
124		}
125		if s.SpecVersion == 0 {
126			s.SpecVersion = SpecCurrentVersion
127		}
128	}
129	
130	// Validate checks that the spec is well-formed after ApplyDefaults has run.
131	func (s *ExecuteLoopSpec) Validate() error {
132		switch s.Mode {
133		case ModeOnce, ModeDrain, ModeWatch:
134		default:
135			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: unknown mode %q (want once, drain, or watch)", s.Mode)
136		}
137		if s.Mode != ModeWatch && s.IdleInterval.Duration != 0 {
138			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: idle_interval is only valid when mode=watch")
139		}
140		if s.SpecVersion != 0 && s.SpecVersion != SpecCurrentVersion {
141			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: unsupported spec_version %d (want %d)", s.SpecVersion, SpecCurrentVersion)
142		}
143		return nil
144	}

5	// ParkReason identifies the canonical reason for transitioning a bead to
6	// proposed status for operator attention. Each constant maps to a fixed
7	// (Reason, Source) pair used by ParkToProposed.
8	type ParkReason string
9	
10	const (
11		ParkIntakeRejection            ParkReason = "intake_rejection"
12		ParkNoChangesOperatorRequired  ParkReason = "no_changes_operator_required"
13		ParkPostReviewMalfunction      ParkReason = "post_review_malfunction"
14		ParkReviewTerminal             ParkReason = "review_terminal"
15		ParkConflictRecovery           ParkReason = "conflict_recovery"
16		ParkReviewRequestClarification ParkReason = "review_request_clarification"
17		ParkLadderExhaustionManual     ParkReason = "ladder_exhaustion_manual"
18	)
19	
20	type parkReasonMeta struct {
21		Reason string
22		Source string
23	}
24	
25	var parkReasonMetaMap = map[ParkReason]parkReasonMeta{
26		ParkIntakeRejection:            {Reason: "pre-claim intake blocked execution", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
27		ParkNoChangesOperatorRequired:  {Reason: "operator decision required before another automated attempt", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
28		ParkPostReviewMalfunction:      {Reason: "review BLOCK triage reached operator-required rung", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
29		ParkReviewTerminal:             {Reason: "terminal review block requires operator decision", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
30		ParkConflictRecovery:           {Reason: "land conflict requires operator judgment", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
31		ParkReviewRequestClarification: {Reason: "reviewer cannot adjudicate needs-judgment AC without operator input", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
32		ParkLadderExhaustionManual:     {Reason: "recovery:manual label set; operator review required", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
33	}
34	
35	// ParkToProposed transitions the bead to proposed status for operator
36	// attention. It enforces OperatorRequired=true and the canonical Reason and
37	// Source from the ParkReason mapping. The mutate callback runs after the
38	// status transition; pass nil if no additional metadata is needed.
39	func (s *Store) ParkToProposed(id string, reason ParkReason, mutate func(*Bead)) error {
40		meta, ok := parkReasonMetaMap[reason]
41		if !ok {
42			return fmt.Errorf("bead: unknown ParkReason %q", reason)
43		}
44		return s.TransitionLifecycle(id, StatusProposed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
45			OperatorRequired: true,
46			Reason:           meta.Reason,
47			Source:           meta.Source,
48		}, func(b *Bead) error {
49			if mutate != nil {
50				mutate(b)
51			}
52			return nil
53		})
54	}
